mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.12% is near zero, compressing equity discount rates and raising present values of future cash flows. This persistently low rate environment mechanically supports S&P 500 valuations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:low rates correlate with elevated equity valuations in post-war US data
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 60% of months when FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25%, or Rank IC for FEDFUNDS level versus forward returns stays below 0.01 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in PAYEMS exceeding 100,000 indicates accelerating job creation, which boosts consumer income and spending expectations, driving corporate earnings and S&P 500 prices higher over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:following the 2009 trough, large payroll gains preceded positive equity returns
failure_condition: When PAYEMS change > 100k, SPX 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year period, or median return is below 0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals a broad-based demand recovery. Consumer spending and residential investment together lift corporate profits and forward-looking equity valuations over a 60-trading-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% and HOUST month-over-month growth > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:in previous recoveries, joint demand growth preceded sustained equity rallies
failure_condition: When both triggers are met, SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (Sharpe ratio < 0.5) over a 3-year period, or the joint signal provides no improvement over using RSAFS alone (Diebold-Mariano test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8% and declines by at least 0.1 percentage points month-over-month, it signals a turning point in the labor market. This early recovery indicator leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price in improving economic fundamentals.
evidence_refs: fact:in the 2009-11 period, unemployment declines from high levels were associated with positive equity returns
failure_condition: When UNRATE declines by >=0.1pp from >8%, SPX 20-day returns are not positive at least 55% of the time over a 2-year period, or the condition yields a negative average return.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in INDPRO exceeding 0.5% signals accelerating industrial activity. Rising production capacity utilization implies stronger corporate profits and economic momentum, pushing S&P 500 prices higher over the subsequent 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production led equity markets in the early phases of post-2009 recovery
failure_condition: When INDPRO rises >0.5%, SPX 5-day forward returns have a negative mean or median, or the proportion of positive 5-day returns is below 50% over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) directly reflects consumer spending strength, which constitutes a large share of GDP. Persistent demand growth lifts corporate earnings and supports rising equity prices over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between RSAFS month-over-month growth and SPX 60-day returns is negative or less than 0.1 over any 5-year rolling window, or the Rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1% and GDP growth is positive, the combination of ultra-loose monetary policy and expanding aggregate demand creates a powerful stimulus for equity valuations. The interaction of low discount rates with improving fundamentals reinforces each other, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:In Q3 2009, GDP returned to positive growth while rates remained near zero
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, SPX 20-day returns are not positive in at least 55% of cases over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression is not significant (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During the early recovery phase when industrial production is still below its pre-crisis peak but beginning to rise each month, positive INDPRO growth signals that supply constraints are easing and economic normalisation is underway. This regime-specific signal drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:In late 2009, INDPRO rose from trough levels, coinciding with equity market gains
regime_hint: early recovery with below-peak industrial output
failure_condition: When INDPRO rises >0.2% from a level below its prior cycle peak, SPX 20-day returns have a negative average or the proportion of positive days is below 50% over a 2-year period.
